file.content:
-- $Id: testes/pm.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

-- UTF-8 file


print('testing pattern matching')

local function checkerror (msg, f, ...)
  local s, err = pcall(f, ...)
  assert(not s and string.find(err, msg))
end


local function f (s, p)
  local i,e = string.find(s, p)
  if i then return string.sub(s, i, e) end
end

local a,b = string.find('', '')    -- empty patterns are tricky
assert(a == 1 and b == 0);
a,b = string.find('alo', '')
assert(a == 1 and b == 0)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a', 1)   -- first position
assert(a == 1 and b == 1)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 2)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 5 and b == 7)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 9)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 9 and b == 11)
a,b = string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', '\0ab', 2);  -- finds at the end
assert(a == 9 and b == 11);
a,b = string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b')    -- last position
assert(a == 11 and b == 11)
assert(not string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b\0'))   -- check ending
assert(not string.find('', '\0'))
assert(string.find('alo123alo', '12') == 4)
assert(not string.find('alo123alo', '^12'))

assert(string.match("aaab", ".*b") == "aaab")
assert(string.match("aaa", ".*a") == "aaa")
assert(string.match("b", ".*b") == "b")

assert(string.match("aaab", ".+b") == "aaab")
assert(string.match("aaa", ".+a") == "aaa")
assert(not string.match("b", ".+b"))

assert(string.match("aaab", ".?b") == "ab")
assert(string.match("aaa", ".?a") == "aa")
assert(string.match("b", ".?b") == "b")

content.text: I see the bug. At line 938, the bounds check is `pos + rest.len() <= haystack.len()` but the slice access needs `pos + 1 + rest.len() <= haystack.len()`. The off-by-one fails on `string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b\0')` where `b` is found at position 10 in an 11-byte haystack, then the slice tries to read `[11..12]`.

delta.text: had an off-by-one in the bounds check before slicing. The check `pos + rest.len() <= haystack.len()` permitted `

pos + 1 + rest.len()` to equal `haystack.len() + 1`, panicking on the slice access at line 939.

**Fix:** Tightened the gu
ard to `pos + 1 + rest.len() <= haystack.len()`. Triggered by `string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b\0
')` (pm.lua:34): the `b` is found at position 10 of an 11-byte haystack, so the trailing-

`\0` check tried to read `haystack[11..12]`.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -q -p
lua-cli` succeeds. Did not re-run the test program per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test.

content.text:
Build is green (only pre-existing warnings).

**Root cause:** `lmemfind` at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:938` had an off-by-one in the bounds check before slicing. The check `pos + rest.len() <= haystack.len()` permitted `pos + 1 + rest.len()` to equal `haystack.len() + 1`, panicking on the slice access at line 939.

**Fix:** Tightened the guard to `pos + 1 + rest.len() <= haystack.len()`. Triggered by `string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b\0')` (pm.lua:34): the `b` is found at position 10 of an 11-byte haystack, so the trailing-`\0` check tried to read `haystack[11..12]`.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` succeeds. Did not re-run the test program per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test.

result:
Build is green (only pre-existing warnings).

**Root cause:** `lmemfind` at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:938` had an off-by-one in the bounds check before slicing. The check `pos + rest.len() <= haystack.len()` permitted `pos + 1 + rest.len()` to equal `haystack.len() + 1`, panicking on the slice access at line 939.

**Fix:** Tightened the guard to `pos + 1 + rest.len() <= haystack.len()`. Triggered by `string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b\0')` (pm.lua:34): the `b` is found at position 10 of an 11-byte haystack, so the trailing-`\0` check tried to read `haystack[11..12]`.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` succeeds. Did not re-run the test program per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test.
